Interface RuleService

All Known Implementing Classes:
JdbcRuleService, MockRuleService
public interface RuleService
Defines operations for managing automation rules in the smart-home system.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Rule
    addRule(String name, String triggerType, String sourceDevice, String condition, String action, String targetDevice)
    Adds a new rule.
    boolean
    deleteRule(String ruleId)
    Deletes a rule.
    boolean
    executeRule(String ruleId)
    Executes a rule in the engine and emits in-app notifications.
    boolean
    executeRule(String ruleId, boolean isManual)
    Executes a rule in the engine and emits in-app notifications.
    javafx.collections.ObservableList<Rule>
    getRules()
    Returns all rules currently managed by the service.
    boolean
    hasConflicts(String ruleId)
    Detects conflicts between rules.
    void
    startRecurringExecution()
    Starts background polling that evaluates all enabled rules on a fixed interval and executes them when their condition is met.
    void
    stopRecurringExecution()
    Stops background polling started by startRecurringExecution().
    boolean
    toggleRule(String ruleId)
    Toggles a rule's enabled state.
    boolean
    updateRule(String ruleId, String name, String triggerType, String sourceDevice, String condition, String action, String targetDevice)
    Updates an existing rule.

  • Method Details

    • getRules

      javafx.collections.ObservableList<Rule> getRules()
      Returns all rules currently managed by the service.
      Returns:
      observable list of rules

    • addRule

      Rule addRule(String name, String triggerType, String sourceDevice, String condition, String action, String targetDevice)
      Adds a new rule.
      Parameters:
      name - display name of the rule
      triggerType - trigger category
      sourceDevice - source device or source label
      condition - trigger condition expression
      action - action to execute
      targetDevice - target device name
      Returns:
      created rule instance, or null when validation fails

    • updateRule

      boolean updateRule(String ruleId, String name, String triggerType, String sourceDevice, String condition, String action, String targetDevice)
      Updates an existing rule.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - identifier of the rule to update
      name - updated rule name
      triggerType - updated trigger category
      sourceDevice - updated source device or source label
      condition - updated trigger condition expression
      action - updated action text
      targetDevice - updated target device name
      Returns:
      true when the rule exists and the update is valid, otherwise false

    • deleteRule

      boolean deleteRule(String ruleId)
      Deletes a rule.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - identifier of the rule to delete
      Returns:
      true when the rule existed and was removed, otherwise false

    • toggleRule

      boolean toggleRule(String ruleId)
      Toggles a rule's enabled state.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - identifier of the rule to toggle
      Returns:
      true when the rule exists and was toggled, otherwise false

    • executeRule

      boolean executeRule(String ruleId)
      Executes a rule in the engine and emits in-app notifications.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - identifier of the rule to execute
      Returns:
      true when execution succeeded, otherwise false

    • executeRule

      boolean executeRule(String ruleId, boolean isManual)
      Executes a rule in the engine and emits in-app notifications. When executed manually, the rule's condition is not evaluated and the action is performed directly.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - identifier of the rule to execute
      isManual - true to skip condition evaluation, false to evaluate condition normally
      Returns:
      true when execution succeeded, otherwise false

    • hasConflicts

      boolean hasConflicts(String ruleId)
      Detects conflicts between rules.
      Parameters:
      ruleId - identifier of the rule being validated
      Returns:
      true when a conflict exists, otherwise false

    • startRecurringExecution

      void startRecurringExecution()
      Starts background polling that evaluates all enabled rules on a fixed interval and executes them when their condition is met. Must be idempotent — calling it a second time is a no-op.

    • stopRecurringExecution

      void stopRecurringExecution()
      Stops background polling started by startRecurringExecution(). Must be idempotent — safe to call even if not started.
